executive_summary: The parent AI diagnostics market is real and fast-growing ($1.94B in 2025, ~21.7% CAGR), but the specific segment of autonomous AI diagnosis for serious conditions WITHOUT physician oversight has a near-zero serviceable market (~$1.2M SOM) due to global regulatory prohibitions, zero qualifying early adopter segments, and patient preference for physician-supervised AI tools.
score_reasoning:
Score: 36/100
- Data Availability: 20/25 (6+ estimates from 3 institutional sources — Grand View Research Jan 2026, Precedence Research Oct 2025, MarketsandMarkets 2024 — all published within last 2 years; estimates converge within 2x at $1.59-1.94B for 2024-2025)
- Market Scale (SOM): 2/25 (SOM ~$1.2M. The autonomous, no-physician-oversight, direct-to-patient segment for serious conditions like cancer/cardiac is effectively regulatory-blocked globally. Only 1 FDA-approved autonomous AI diagnostic exists — IDx-DR for diabetic retinopathy, a non-serious screening condition. SAM of ~$58M derived from cardiology+oncology share of TAM × 10% direct-to-patient fraction, then SOM at 2% of SAM. Actual obtainable market is arguably near zero given no legal pathway in any major jurisdiction.)
- Growth Trajectory: 14/25 (Median CAGR ~21.7% from 3 institutional sources for the parent AI diagnostics market — solidly in the 10-24% tier. However, this growth is entirely in physician-supervised decision-support tools, not autonomous diagnosis. The specific autonomous segment has no measurable growth trend.)
- Early Adopter Identifiability: 0/25 (0 segments meeting all 3 criteria. While communities exist for health IT professionals — r/healthIT 51k members, HIMSS 25k+ attendees — and patient self-diagnosers — r/AskDocs 1M+ members — none meet the three-criteria test for THIS specific product: autonomous AI diagnosis of serious conditions without physician oversight. Health IT buyers purchase physician-supervised tools. Patients express lower willingness to pay for AI-only diagnosis. No identifiable group that both wants AND can legally access this specific product.)
Total: 20 + 2 + 14 + 0 = 36

tam.source: Precedence Research (Oct 2025), corroborated by Grand View Research ($1.59B in 2024, $9.68B by 2033, Jan 2026) and MarketsandMarkets ($1.71B in 2024)

sam.source: Calculated: TAM × 30% (cardiology+oncology segments per Grand View Research diagnosis segmentation) × 10% (direct-to-patient/consumer fraction vs. B2B hospital purchasing, which dominates 90%+ of current AI diagnostics spend)

som.source: SAM × 2% (conservative capture rate for $1B-$10B parent TAM). Actual SOM likely near zero: (1) no regulatory pathway for autonomous diagnosis of serious conditions in any major market, (2) only 1 FDA-approved autonomous AI diagnostic exists globally (IDx-DR for diabetic retinopathy only), (3) patients express significantly lower willingness to pay for AI-only vs physician-supervised diagnosis, (4) Babylon Health, the most aggressive direct-to-patient AI diagnostics company, collapsed in 2023 after $2B+ losses

data.growth_source: Median of Grand View Research (21.74% CAGR 2026-2033), Precedence Research (20.37% CAGR 2025-2034), MarketsandMarkets (22.5% CAGR 2024-2029)

evidence.quote: The global artificial intelligence in diagnostics market size is expected to reach USD 9.68 billion by 2033, registering a CAGR of 21.74% from 2026 to 2033. Market was estimated at USD 1.59 billion in 2024.

The global artificial intelligence in diagnostics market size was estimated at USD 1.94 billion in 2025 and is anticipated to reach around USD 10.28 billion by 2034, expanding at a CAGR of 20.37% from 2025 to 2034.

The global AI in medical diagnostics market, valued at US$1.33 billion in 2023, stood at US$1.71 billion in 2024 and is projected to advance at a resilient CAGR of 22.5% from 2024 to 2029, culminating in a forecasted valuation of US$4.72 billion.
